# LedeWire JS SDK

> Official JavaScript/TypeScript SDK for the LedeWire API.
> Source: https://github.com/ledewire/ledewire-js-sdk
> Docs: https://ledewire.github.io/ledewire-js-sdk/
> API Reference: https://ledewire.github.io/ledewire-js-sdk/api/

## Packages

- `@ledewire/browser` — Browser SDK (ESM + CDN IIFE `Ledewire` global). Buyer flows only.
- `@ledewire/node` — Node.js SDK. Full API: merchant, seller, and buyer flows.

## Browser CDN usage

```html
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@ledewire/browser@1/dist/ledewire.min.js"></script>
<script>
  const lw = Ledewire.init({ apiKey: 'your_api_key' })
  const state = await lw.checkout.state('content-id')
  // state.checkout_state.next_required_action:
  //   'authenticate' | 'fund_wallet' | 'purchase' | 'view_content'
</script>
```

## Node.js usage

```ts
import { createClient } from '@ledewire/node'
const client = createClient({
  apiKey: process.env.LEDEWIRE_API_KEY,
  apiSecret: process.env.LEDEWIRE_API_SECRET,
})
```

## Client namespaces

### @ledewire/browser — init()
- `lw.config.getPublic()` → `PublicConfigResponse` — public platform config, **no auth required** (use to get `google_client_id` before sign-in)
- `lw.auth.signup(body)` — create buyer account
- `lw.auth.loginWithEmail({ email, password })` — buyer email login
- `lw.auth.loginWithGoogle({ id_token })` — buyer Google login
- `lw.auth.requestPasswordReset({ email })` → `AuthPasswordResetResponse` — send password reset code to buyer email
- `lw.auth.resetPassword({ email, reset_code, password })` → `AuthPasswordResetResponse` — complete password reset with code
- `lw.checkout.state(contentId)` → `CheckoutStateResponse` — next action for content
- `lw.wallet.balance()` → `{ balance_cents }` — buyer wallet balance
- `lw.wallet.transactions()` → list of wallet transactions
- `lw.wallet.createPaymentSession({ amount_cents })` → Stripe payment session
- `lw.wallet.getPaymentStatus(sessionId)` → payment status
- `lw.purchases.create({ content_id })` → create purchase
- `lw.purchases.list()` → list purchases
- `lw.purchases.get(id)` → single purchase
- `lw.content.getWithAccess(contentId)` → content + access info (authenticated user implicit from token)
- `lw.seller.loginWithApiKey({ key, secret? })` — seller API key login (key-only = view; key+secret = full)
- `lw.seller.content.list()` → `ContentResponse[]` — list store content (requires API key login)
- `lw.seller.content.search({ title?, uri?, metadata? })` → `ContentResponse[]` — search store content
- `lw.seller.content.get(contentId)` → `ContentResponse` — get single content item

### @ledewire/node — createClient()
- `client.config.getPublic()` → `PublicConfigResponse` — public platform config, **no auth required** (use to get `google_client_id` before sign-in)
- `client.auth.*` — buyer auth (signup, email, google, logout, password reset)
- `client.merchant.auth.loginWithEmail({ email, password })` — merchant email login
- `client.merchant.auth.loginWithGoogle({ id_token })` — merchant Google login
- `client.merchant.auth.listStores()` → `ManageableStore[]` — stores the merchant can manage
- `client.merchant.auth.requestPasswordReset({ email })` → `MerchantPasswordResetResponse` — send password reset code to merchant email
- `client.merchant.auth.resetPassword({ email, reset_code, password })` → `MerchantPasswordResetResponse` — complete merchant password reset with code
- `client.merchant.users.list(storeId)` → team members
- `client.merchant.users.invite(storeId, body)` → invite team member
- `client.merchant.users.remove(storeId, userId)` → remove team member
- `client.merchant.sales.summary(storeId)` → `SalesSummaryResponse`
- `client.merchant.sales.list(storeId)` → `SalesStatisticsItem[]`
- `client.merchant.sales.get(storeId, saleId)` → `MerchantSaleResponse`
- `client.merchant.buyers.list(storeId)` → `BuyerStatisticsItem[]`
- `client.merchant.config.get(storeId)` → `StoreConfig` (store-level config; requires merchant auth)
- `client.seller.content.*` — content CRUD + search
- `client.wallet.*` — balance, transactions, createPaymentSession, getPaymentStatus
- `client.purchases.*` — create, list, get
- `client.content.getWithAccess(contentId, userId?)` → content + access info (`userId` for merchant proxy lookups)
- `client.checkout.state(contentId)` → `CheckoutStateResponse`

## Key types (packages/core)

```ts
interface CheckoutStateResponse {
  content_id: string
  content_title: string
  price_cents: number
  checkout_state: {
    is_authenticated: boolean
    has_sufficient_funds: boolean | null
    has_purchased: boolean
    next_required_action: 'authenticate' | 'fund_wallet' | 'purchase' | 'view_content'
  }
}

// All SDK errors extend LedewireError
class LedewireError extends Error { statusCode: number; code: number | undefined }
class AuthError extends LedewireError {}       // statusCode 401 — bad credentials or expired token
class ForbiddenError extends LedewireError {}  // statusCode 403 — authenticated but wrong role
class NotFoundError extends LedewireError {}   // statusCode 404
class PurchaseError extends LedewireError {}   // statusCode 409/422
```

## Merchant auth error distinction (important)

`merchant.auth.loginWithEmail` and `merchant.auth.loginWithGoogle` throw
`ForbiddenError` (403), **not** `AuthError` (401), when the credentials are
valid but the account has no merchant store associations — for example, a
personal Google account previously registered as a buyer.

```ts
try {
  await client.merchant.auth.loginWithGoogle({ id_token })
} catch (err) {
  if (err instanceof ForbiddenError) {
    // err.message → "This account does not have merchant access. Use a merchant or owner account."
    // Fix: use an account that is an owner or author on at least one store.
  } else if (err instanceof AuthError) {
    // Bad or expired credentials.
  }
}
```

The same pattern applies to `loginWithEmailAndListStores` and
`loginWithGoogleAndListStores`.

## Token storage adapter interface

```ts
interface TokenStorage {
  getTokens(): Promise<StoredTokens | null>
  setTokens(tokens: StoredTokens): Promise<void>
  clearTokens(): Promise<void>
}

interface StoredTokens {
  accessToken: string
  refreshToken: string
  expiresAt: number  // Unix timestamp (ms)
}
```

Pass a custom `storage` to `createClient()` or `init()` to persist sessions
(e.g. to Redis or a database). The default is `MemoryTokenStorage` (in-memory).

For browsers, `localStorageAdapter()` and `sessionStorageAdapter()` from
`@ledewire/browser` persist sessions across page reloads. Use
`sessionStorageAdapter` to scope tokens to a single tab (recommended for
embedded widgets):

```ts
import { init, localStorageAdapter, sessionStorageAdapter } from '@ledewire/browser'
const lw = init({ apiKey: 'your_api_key', storage: localStorageAdapter() })
// or, for tab-scoped sessions:
const lw = init({ apiKey: 'your_api_key', storage: sessionStorageAdapter() })
```

## Architecture notes

- `packages/core` is private (not published). Shared by browser and node packages.
- `TokenManager` handles JWT refresh automatically — do NOT manually refresh.
- HTTP errors are mapped to typed error classes in `packages/core/src/errors.ts`.
- OpenAPI source of truth: `ledewire.yml` at the repo root.
- All exported public symbols have JSDoc (enforced by ESLint `jsdoc/require-jsdoc`).
- Tests use Vitest + MSW for HTTP mocking. Coverage ≥ 90% enforced in CI.
